reasoning: IPG is in the process of being acquired by Omnicom (OMC) in an all-stock deal expected to close in November 2025. Given the FTC's approval on September 27, 2025, and the pending closure, IPG's stock price acts primarily as a proxy for OMC's stock price, augmented by a very small, shrinking merger arbitrage spread. Over a short 5-day trading window (from the Friday prior to Nov 9 to the Friday prior to Nov 16), broad stock market movements follow a near-random walk. While short-term movements are almost a coin flip, general equity risk premiums and the amortizing nature of the merger arbitrage spread contribute to a slight positive drift in the expectation. Based on the standard log-normal model of stock prices, a 1-week horizon corresponds to roughly a 51-53% chance of closing higher. The scheduled release of Q3 earnings on November 10 is unlikely to induce a directional bias, as it is already anticipated by the market and no conference call will be held due to the merger. Therefore, my probability is set to 52%, reflecting this slight structural positive bias over a one-week horizon in an efficient market.
